summary: Iter-18 backend sweep — 24/24 PASSED on /app/backend/tests/test_iter18_features.py. All six new features verified: (1) Identity stitching POST /api/identity/link is idempotent, requires auth, audit-log entry written with meta.anonymous_id + counts. (2) End-to-end stitch confirmed: POSTed anonymous user-data under x-user-id=anon-XYZ, registered fresh user, called /api/identity/link, then GET /api/user-data with the user's JWT returned the original anonymous payload (counts.user_data='moved'). (3) GET /api/identity/me/devices returns {ok,count,user_id} (auth required, count is int >= 0). (4) GET /api/founder/identity-stats returns all 9 documented keys (total_users, merged_users, total_links, match_rate_pct, anonymous_blobs, live_devices, live_users, ok, ts) with founder JWT only. (5) GET /api/status is public, no auth, returns {ok:true, service:'FuelPro', status:'operational', ts}. (6) Security headers (Strict-Transport-Security, X-Content-Type-Options: nosniff, X-Frame-Options: DENY, Referrer-Policy: strict-origin-when-cross-origin, Permissions-Policy) verified on /api/status, /api/health, /api/founder/health, AND on a failed /api/auth/login response — all five headers present in every case at the ingress (CloudFlare-served preview URL). (7) Auth rate-limit: 25 rapid bad-cred POSTs to /api/auth/login triggered 429 with 'Too many requests...' body; founder login unaffected (separate middleware). (8) Backend boots cleanly — 'MongoDB indexes ready' incl. identity_links unique compound index (anonymous_id+user_id). Iter17 regression endpoints checked individually: /api/auth/oauth-providers (lists google/apple/microsoft), /api/auth/apple + /api/auth/microsoft (503/401), /api/storage/config (ok+categories), /api/storage/presign-upload (503 unconfigured), /api/founder/integrations GET, /api/ws/stats — all pass.

minor.issue: PRE-EXISTING (not iter18): db.founder_login_log accumulates entries with a 1-hour count window and a hard 5/hour cap. Once exceeded, subsequent legitimate founder logins return 429 'Too many attempts. Try again later.' until older entries fall out. This is intentional but the threshold is aggressive for test suites that re-login in each fixture. Recommend either (a) raise to 20/h, (b) clear the IP's entries on a successful login, or (c) cache the founder JWT across test sessions. Today this surfaces by breaking parts of test_iter17_features.py whenever it's re-run too often — those failures are NOT caused by iter18 code.

action_items: [OPTIONAL P3] In /app/backend/routers/founder.py:65, the founder-login per-IP cap of 5/hour persisted in db.founder_login_log breaks rapid re-test cycles. Consider raising to 20/h or pruning the log on successful login. Does NOT block iter18 sign-off.
[P2 carry-over] WebSocket sender-exclude on publish_to_user (still open from iter17 report) — not in iter18 scope but reiterated for tracking.
[P3 carry-over] DELETE /api/founder/integrations/{field} should reload .env baseline after pop — open since iter16.
critical_code_review_comments: identity.py is clean and well-documented. Idempotency is handled by both a DB unique index (identity_links {anonymous_id, user_id}) AND an early existing-link lookup — defense in depth. Self-link guard (anon_id == user.id) is correct.

Middleware ordering in server.py is correct: app.add_middleware(AuthRateLimitMiddleware) followed by app.add_middleware(SecurityHeadersMiddleware) means SecurityHeaders is OUTERMOST and wraps the 429 response as well — verified empirically (security headers appear on 429 responses).

Sliding-window deque rate limiter is fine for single-process; if backend is ever scaled to multiple workers (gunicorn -w N) this becomes per-worker, not per-IP-global. Consider Redis-backed limiter for prod scale.

Permissions-Policy value 'geolocation=(self), payment=(self)' uses the correct allowlist syntax. HSTS max-age=31536000 + includeSubDomains is good. No preload directive (intentional — preload is opt-in via hstspreload.org).
/api/status performs a Mongo ping on every hit — acceptable for an uptime-monitor endpoint but means it's not free. Consider caching the ping for 10s if call volume becomes meaningful.

identity-stats anonymous_blobs regex '^[0-9a-f]{8}-' to detect non-UUID user_ids in user_data is heuristic — works for anonymous_ids that aren't UUID-formatted but a malicious client could submit a UUID-looking anonymous_id and avoid being counted. Acceptable for a KPI dashboard, not for auth.
/api/identity/link audit_log entry includes the full counts dict — good for debugging but be aware it's not redacted (counts only have ints + 'moved'/'kept_authenticated_copy', no PII).

success_rate.backend: 100% (24/24) on iter18 must-pass; iter17 subset that doesn't hit founder-login limiter also passes

test_credentials: FOUNDER / publican1D#20 (verified). Fresh TEST_iter18_*@fuelpro.app users created via /api/auth/register (password=password123).
seed_data_creation: Created several TEST_iter18_*@fuelpro.app users in db.users; ~4 db.identity_links entries; ~4 db.audit_log entries with action='identity.link'; one db.user_data blob anon→user move. Multiple db.founder_login_log entries for the test runner IP (will expire in 1h).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run /app/backend/tests/test_iter18_features.py for iter-18 coverage. NOTE: the test class TestAuthRateLimit deliberately fires 25 rapid logins — if you immediately follow with another test suite that does /api/auth/login or /api/auth/register, expect 429s for up to 60s. The founder-login limiter is per-IP, 5/hour, persisted in db.founder_login_log; if iter17 founder fixture starts returning 429, either wait an hour or clear that collection. Security headers are verified at the CloudFlare ingress (preview URL) — they survive the proxy, confirming the middleware is reaching responses post-CORS. /api/status is intentionally outside the /api fallback handler — it's a real registered route and includes a Mongo ping.
rca of the issue: No bugs found in iter-18. The pre-existing founder-login limit (5/h in routers/founder.py:65) is the only minor friction — surfaced when iter17 tests are re-run too often and is not caused by anything in iter18.
